water.name: Santa Margarita River and tributaries downstream from the Interstate Highway 15 bridge
aliases: Santa Margarita River (San Diego and Riverside Cos.)

authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40(b)(97) — Santa Margarita River and tributaries downstream from the Interstate Highway 15 bridge (San Diego and Riverside Cos.)
sources.quotedText: (97) Santa Margarita River and tributaries downstream from the Interstate Highway 15 bridge (San Diego and Riverside Cos.). Closed to all fishing all year.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 273559, "Santa Margarita River", Stream, Riverside/San Diego Cos., CA
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":273559,"gaz_name":"Santa Margarita River","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"Riverside"}; geometry multipoint, last point (Web Mercator -13040031.8045, 3958416.5827) converts to lon -117.140599, lat 33.474200, at the river's source (confluence of Murrieta Creek and Temecula Creek near the Interstate 15 bridge in Temecula), matching Wikipedia's infobox source coordinate (33°28'27"N 117°08'26"W = 33.47417, -117.14056). Corrects the file's prior coordinate (-117.1500, 33.4800), which was off by ~1.1 km.

parameters.note: Closed to all fishing all year on the Santa Margarita River and tributaries downstream from the Interstate Highway 15 bridge. The Santa Margarita is a high-priority recovery river for the endangered Southern California steelhead under NMFS's Southern Steelhead Recovery Plan.

regulations.humanSummary: Closed to all fishing all year
regulations.verbatimText: Closed to all fishing all year.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
